items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against First Solar, Inc. alleging that company defendants made materially false and misleading statements regarding the company's ability to manage U.S. tariff policy impacts. The complaint claims defendants overstated capacity and understated negative effects from production facility underutilization in Malaysia and Vietnam, as well as U.S. relocation efforts, which would harm 2026 fiscal year performance. Investors who purchased First Solar securities between February 26, 2025 and February 24, 2026 are encouraged to join the case.

items.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Zoetis Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. alleging securities fraud for materially false and misleading statements regarding its Companion Animal segment products. The complaint claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of Librela due to FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses for Simparica Trio to lower-priced competitors, and declining market share for dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. Investors who purchased Zoetis securities between January 14, 2025 and May 6, 2026 are eligible to join the class action, with a lead plaintiff deadline of July 27, 2026.

Major customers like Oracle and partnerships with Brookfield Asset Management validate the technology, with strong Q1 financials showing 130% revenue growth. items.title: SPR Wins Fifth Consecutive Northeast Ohio Top Workplaces Award
items.summary: SPR, a medical device company specializing in peripheral nerve stimulation (PNS) for pain management, has been named a Top Workplace by Northeast Ohio Top Workplaces for the fifth consecutive year. The recognition reflects the company's strong workplace culture amid significant growth, including doubled employee count and surpassed 50,000 patients treated with its SPRINT PNS System. SPR announced that Medtronic intends to acquire the company, a partnership expected to expand the SPRINT PNS reach to more patients.

items.title: Orchestra BioMed Joins Russell 3000® and Russell 2000® Indexes
items.summary: Orchestra BioMed Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: OBIO) announced its inclusion in the Russell 3000 Index and Russell 2000 Index following the 2026 Russell indexes reconstitution effective June 26, 2026. The biomedical company develops AVIM Therapy for hypertension in collaboration with Medtronic and Virtue SAB for atherosclerotic artery disease, both undergoing pivotal clinical trials.

items.title: Block & Leviton LLP Announces Proposed Class Action Settlement on Behalf of Purchasers of Common Stock of Biogen, Inc. – BIIB
items.summary: A federal court has approved an $18.9 million settlement for investors who purchased Biogen Inc. common stock between June 8, 2021 and July 12, 2021. A settlement hearing is scheduled for September 29, 2026, to determine if the settlement is fair and reasonable. Class members must submit a Proof of Claim by September 24, 2026 to receive compensation.

items.title: SpaceX's $60 Billion Cursor Acquisition Changes Everything. Here's Why.
items.summary: SpaceX announced a $60 billion acquisition of Anysphere, developer of the AI-powered code editor Cursor, to be paid entirely in new shares. The deal aims to strengthen SpaceX's AI capabilities, which the company views as its largest addressable market at $28.5 trillion. While SpaceX's AI division (xAI) generated $818 million in revenue in Q1 2026, it reported a $2.5 billion operating loss. Cursor brings $4 billion in annualized revenue and enterprise deals with companies like Nvidia.

items.title: How Buying Penguin Solutions Today Could 10X Your Net Worth
items.summary: Penguin Solutions, a memory and AI infrastructure company, shows promise despite a 6% year-over-year revenue decline. The decline is attributed to legacy business slowdown, while its high-growth integrated memory segment surged 63% YoY, driven by AI and high-performance computing customer deals. The company recently received Nvidia's AI factory partner designation, positioning it for potential significant growth.

items.title: AI’s Hidden Cost Problem Is Making Memory Chip Makers the Biggest Winners
items.summary: As AI infrastructure buildout accelerates, memory chip suppliers (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix) have become critical bottlenecks, commanding pricing power similar to oil producers during an airline boom. While hyperscalers absorb rising memory costs to maintain competitive advantage, the economics remain unsustainable long-term. The AI profit pool is fragmenting, with memory suppliers capturing disproportionate gains until efficiency improvements, better monetization, or new supply capacity arrives.

items.title: Oxidative Stress Assay Market Size to Reach USD 3.25 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global Oxidative Stress Assay Market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 9.53% from 2026 to 2035, driven by rising chronic disease prevalence, pharmaceutical R&D investments, and advancements in biomarker diagnostics. North America leads with 38.52% market share, while Asia-Pacific is expected to show the highest growth rate at 10.55%. ELISA technology dominates with 39% market share, and cancer research accounts for 33% of revenue.

items.title: Password Management Market to Reach $42.21 Billion by 2035 as Cyberattacks and Identity Security Risks Drive Enterprise Adoption | Report by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global password management market is projected to grow from $5.55 billion in 2025 to $42.21 billion by 2035, driven by rising cyberattacks, data breaches, remote work expansion, and regulatory compliance requirements. The U.S. market is expected to reach $12.53 billion while Europe reaches $2.63 billion by 2035. Large enterprises currently dominate the market, with BFSI and healthcare sectors leading adoption.

items.title: ZOETIS DEADLINE: ROSEN, RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Zoetis Inc. Investors with Losses in Excess of $100K to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – ZTS
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about its Companion Animal products during January 2025 to May 2026. The lawsuit claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of its Librela pain treatment following FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses in Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining market share in dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. Investors with losses exceeding $100,000 are encouraged to join the class action before the July 27, 2026 deadline.

items.title: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages Embecta Corp. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – EMBC
items.summary: Rosen Law Firm has filed class action lawsuits against Embecta Corp. for allegedly making false statements about fiscal results and pen needle business performance, cutting 2026 guidance. The firm is also investigating claims against Zoetis Inc. and Disc Medicine Inc. Investors who purchased securities during specified periods may be eligible for compensation.

items.title: Tesla Reports Q2 Deliveries in a Matter of Days. Here's the Number That Matters.
items.summary: Tesla is set to report Q2 vehicle deliveries in early July. Wall Street expects around 406,000 deliveries, which would represent year-over-year growth above Q2 2025's 384,122 vehicles. The key question is whether strength in Europe and China can offset softness in the U.S. market due to expired tax credits. While Tesla's valuation is driven by robotaxi and AI ambitions, the automotive business remains critical as it generates the majority of revenue.

items.title: If AI Is Such a Sure Thing, Why Are So Many Insiders Selling?
items.summary: Despite soaring public enthusiasm for AI investments, prominent insiders including Stanley Druckenmiller, Peter Thiel, and Michael Burry are quietly selling or hedging their AI-related positions. Industry experts warn the AI bubble could be more explosive than the dot-com peak, raising questions about whether insider actions reflect rational caution about future expectations.

items.title: The Case For and Against Buying Chevron Right Now
items.summary: Chevron offers an attractive 4.1% dividend yield and a strong track record of dividend increases, making it appealing for energy exposure. However, Middle East geopolitical tensions are driving volatile oil prices based on investor emotions rather than fundamentals. The company warns that industry fundamentals are weaker than perceived, suggesting investors may want to wait for calmer conditions before buying.

items.title: Is Micron Stock the New Nvidia?
items.summary: Micron Technology has emerged as a major AI beneficiary, providing essential memory and storage products for AI systems. With revenue quadrupling to over $41 billion and net income reaching $28 billion in the latest quarter, Micron's stock has surged over 800% in the past year. While the company faces competition from SK Hynix in AI memory, strong demand exceeding supply and high gross margins (84%) position Micron similarly to Nvidia's growth trajectory, though with potentially more risk.

items.title: McCormick & Company, Incorporated(NYSE: MKC) added to Russell 1000 Dynamic Index - marketscreener.com
items.summary: McCormick & Company, Incorporated(NYSE: MKC) added to Russell 1000 Dynamic Index    marketscreener.com

items.title: ROSEN, GLOBAL INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Microsoft Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – MSFT
items.summary: Rosen Law Firm has filed a securities class action lawsuit against Microsoft on behalf of investors who purchased MSFT stock between May 1, 2025 and January 28, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Microsoft made false statements regarding its Copilot AI products, claiming the company failed to disclose significant technical problems, underperformance versus competitors, and the need for billions in additional capital expenditures that diverted resources from profitable Azure services. The lead plaintiff deadline is August 11, 2026.

items.title: This Nvidia-Backed Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stock Has Multibagger Potential. It Is Trading at an Incredibly Attractive Valuation Right Now
items.summary: CoreWeave, an AI infrastructure provider backed by Nvidia's $2 billion investment, is positioned for significant growth with a $99.4 billion revenue backlog and plans to expand data center capacity to 8 GW by 2030. Trading at 8x sales with triple-digit revenue growth, the company could potentially become a multibagger if it achieves projected $40 billion revenue by 2028.

items.title: 1 Stock That's More Than Doubled in 3 Years, and 3 Reasons It Will Keep Soaring
items.summary: General Motors stock has surged 116% over three years and 62% in the past year, outperforming the S&P 500. The article highlights three reasons for continued growth: aggressive share buybacks resulting in a 7.6% total shareholder yield, a growing software business through OnStar and Super Cruise subscriptions with high-margin recurring revenue, and progress toward EV profitability through LMR battery technology expected within three to five years.

items.title: Is GameStop the Next Berkshire Hathaway?
items.summary: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen has revived the struggling retailer and amassed nearly $7.4 billion in cash, leading to comparisons with Berkshire Hathaway. However, the article argues GameStop is not the next Berkshire Hathaway due to fundamental differences: GameStop lacks insurance float like Berkshire, and Cohen's activist investment approach differs from Buffett's long-term strategy. Cohen's attempted acquisition of eBay represents empire-building rather than Berkshire-style investing.

items.title: Meta Platforms vs. Snap: Comparing Revenue Scale and Recent Trajectories
items.summary: Meta Platforms significantly outpaces Snap in revenue generation and profitability, with Meta reporting $56.3 billion in Q1 2026 revenue (33% YoY growth) and a 48% net income margin, while Snap generated only $1.5 billion (12% YoY growth) with a -6% net margin. Meta's substantial AI investments are driving growth, whereas Snap lacks comparable capacity to invest in AI development, potentially hindering its future competitiveness.

items.title: 1 Unstoppable Trend That Could Supercharge Ford Stock by 2030
items.summary: Ford is pivoting from struggling EV battery production to manufacturing battery energy storage systems (BESS) for AI data centers, which face critical power supply shortages. The company launched Ford Energy subsidiary and plans to invest $2 billion to retool its Kentucky facility, targeting 20 GWh annual capacity by 2027. A $4 billion deal with EDF Power Solutions could drive significant revenue growth and potentially warrant a valuation rerating for the automaker.

items.title: Don't Buy SpaceX Until You Consider These 2 Aerospace and Defense Stocks With 10% EPS Growth
items.summary: The article argues that Howmet Aerospace and TransDigm Group are more attractive aerospace and defense investments than SpaceX, citing their strong EPS growth profiles, high-margin aftermarket businesses, and disciplined acquisition strategies. Both companies benefit from aging aircraft fleets requiring maintenance and possess significant pricing power through proprietary products and sole-source supplier relationships.

items.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Cloud Stock Has Crushed Amazon, Microsoft, and Google in 2026. It Can Continue Skyrocketing After 184% Gains
items.summary: DigitalOcean's stock has surged 184% in 2026, significantly outperforming Amazon, Microsoft, and Google. The company is capitalizing on strong demand for AI cloud computing by offering simplified, cost-effective solutions tailored for small and medium-sized businesses. With AI-focused annual recurring revenue jumping 221% year-over-year and inference services growing 487%, DigitalOcean projects 26% revenue growth in 2026 and over 50% in 2027, suggesting substantial upside potential despite its premium valuation.

items.title: A Former CIA Advisor Says This Tech Boom Is Already Bigger Than the Internet Ever Was
items.summary: Financial researcher Jim Rickards warns that the $5 trillion AI data center buildout in the U.S. may mirror the dot-com bubble, with companies creating circular revenue streams through chip and cloud capacity sales. Unlike the internet boom, AI faces physical and energy constraints, with experts suggesting diminishing returns on compute investments. Rickards highlights July 29th Meta earnings as a potential turning point, comparing it to March 2000 when a Barron's article triggered the Nasdaq's 80% decline.

items.title: Most Investors Have Never Heard of This Industrial Stock. That's About to Change.
items.summary: EMCOR Group, a construction and engineering services provider, has experienced significant growth driven by the AI data center boom. The company's revenues grew from $12.6 billion in 2023 to nearly $17 billion by 2025, with earnings more than doubling. Q1 2026 showed 19.7% year-over-year revenue growth and 30% earnings growth, prompting management to raise full-year guidance. Trading at around $845 per share (up from $175 in mid-2023), the stock trades at 28.5x forward earnings and is positioned for continued growth as data center construction demand remains strong.

items.title: Got $200? Here's What Buying 1 Share of Each of These 3 Stocks on the Dip Could Look Like in 5 Years.
items.summary: Three consumer growth stocks—Dutch Bros, Chipotle, and Cava—are trading below recent highs due to margin pressure and macroeconomic concerns rather than fundamental deterioration. The article suggests a $200 basket portfolio approach across all three could be an attractive long-term compounding opportunity for patient investors with a 5+ year horizon.

items.title: As SpaceX Falls Down to Earth, the Stock Is Still Not a Buy
items.summary: SpaceX's stock has pulled back to opening-day levels after an initial strong start following its IPO as the largest company ever to go public. The article warns investors to avoid the stock due to upcoming lock-up expirations that could pressure shares, a high valuation relative to revenue, and heavy reliance on speculative future ventures like space-based AI data centers.

items.title: The AI Memory Supercycle Could Last Longer Than Investors Expect. That's Great News for Micron and Sandisk.
items.summary: Micron's Q3 FY2026 earnings significantly exceeded expectations with $41.5B in revenue versus $33.5B guidance, suggesting the AI-driven memory chip supercycle is still in early stages and likely to extend longer than anticipated. The company's record-level investments and multiyear customer agreements indicate sustained demand, while Sandisk is expected to report similarly strong results. These developments suggest the memory supercycle will maintain elevated prices and high margins for extended periods.

items.summary: How Truist’s CEO Transition and Stress Test Results Could Shape Truist Financial (TFC) Investors’ Outlook    simplywall.st

items.title: You Can Buy 1 Share of Costco for Less Than $1,000 Right Now. Here's Why the Price Could Go Up by the End of 2030.
items.summary: Despite trading at premium valuation ratios (48x trailing earnings and 48x free cash flow), Costco's stock is positioned for continued growth through 2030. The warehouse club's 92.2% membership renewal rate, high-margin membership fee income, planned expansion of 30+ warehouses annually, and successful tech investments justify its premium pricing compared to discount retail peers.

items.title: CHX DEADLINE: ROSEN, RECOGNIZED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages ChampionX Corporation Investors with Losses in Excess of $100K to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - CHX
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against ChampionX Corporation alleging that defendants failed to disclose material information about acquisition offers from Schlumberger Limited while simultaneously repurchasing company stock at artificially depressed prices. The lawsuit covers the period from February 29, 2024 to April 1, 2024. Investors with losses exceeding $100,000 are encouraged to join the class action before the July 14, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline.

items.title: מועד אחרון לתביעה ייצוגית נגד CHX: רוזן, יועץ למשקיעים ידוע, מעודד משקיעים ב- ChampionX Corporation עם הפסדים של יותר מ-100 אלף דולר לקבל ייעוץ משפטי לפני המועד האחרון החשוב בתביעה ייצוגית לניירות ערך - CHX
items.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is reminding ChampionX Corporation (NASDAQ: CHX) shareholders who sold stock between February 29, 2024 and April 1, 2024 of the July 14, 2026 deadline to join a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that ChampionX failed to disclose a merger offer from Schlumberger Limited and engaged in stock buybacks at artificially depressed prices during the undisclosed negotiation period. The merger was eventually completed on July 16, 2025 at $40.58 per share, while the average stock price during the class period was $33.32.

items.title: Micron Just Revealed a Massive Multi-Decade Growth Opportunity. It's Not AI Data Centers. It Might Be Even Bigger.
items.summary: Micron's CEO highlighted humanoid robots and autonomous vehicles as a massive multi-decade growth opportunity for memory and storage demand. Humanoid robots require 10 times more memory than advanced autonomous vehicles. Bank of America predicts 300 million robots globally by 2040 and 3 billion by 2060, potentially creating sustained demand that could reduce Micron's cyclical nature.

items.title: A CFO Departure. A Clinical Miss. A $43 Billion Question Mark. Should You Throw in the Towel on Pfizer Stock?
items.summary: Pfizer faces mounting challenges including a failed phase 3 clinical trial for cancer drug sigvotatug vedotin and the departure of CFO Dave Denton. Despite these setbacks and the $43 billion Seagen acquisition, the article argues the stock remains attractive due to strong pipeline candidates like MET-097i in weight loss, successful products like Padcev and Abrysvo, and a solid 7.3% dividend yield.

items.title: 2 Nvidia-Owned Stocks Investors Should Buy Now
items.summary: Nvidia's investment portfolio includes CoreWeave and Nokia, two companies positioned to benefit from AI growth. CoreWeave, a neocloud provider, has a $99 billion backlog and 112% revenue growth but carries significant debt concerns. Nokia, pivoting to AI-enabled telecom equipment through its Nvidia partnership, shows modest revenue growth but has surged 170% over the past year, with potential upside as 5G and 6G deployments accelerate.

items.title: Nike at a 12-Year Low or Lululemon at an 8-Year Low? Here's the Better Turnaround Stock for Deep Value Investors to Buy in July.
items.summary: Both Nike and Lululemon are struggling with sales pressures and leadership changes, but Nike emerges as the better turnaround opportunity for value investors. Nike shows early recovery signs with flat year-over-year sales and strong brand power, while offering a 3.9% dividend yield. Lululemon trades at a cheaper valuation (P/E of 9 vs Nike's 28) but faces greater uncertainty with interim leadership and a new CEO starting in September.

items.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Has Dropped 13% in 1 Month. Here's Why It's a Buy
items.summary: Alphabet's stock has declined 13% over the past month due to concerns about massive AI-related capital spending ($180-190 billion in capex this year) and the departure of key AI talent. However, the company's AI investments are showing strong returns, with Google Cloud revenue up 63% year-over-year and generative AI product sales growing nearly 800%. The company maintains a strong competitive moat through its dominant search business and YouTube platform, making it an attractive buying opportunity.

items.title: 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks I'd Buy Now and Never Sell
items.summary: The article recommends three AI stocks for long-term investment: Arm Holdings for its energy-efficient chip architecture dominating data centers, IREN for its data center capacity expansion with major contracts from Microsoft and Nvidia, and Nvidia for its leadership in AI hardware and diversified revenue streams. With Morgan Stanley projecting $3 trillion in global data center construction costs through 2028, these companies are positioned to benefit from sustained AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: Millions of Investors Are About to Own SpaceX Stock Indirectly -- Whether They Want to or Not
items.summary: SpaceX has been added to the Russell 1000 and will join the Nasdaq-100, forcing millions of index fund investors to own the stock automatically. However, the company remains unprofitable and trades at over 100x revenue, while the S&P 500 is keeping it out due to lack of profitability. The addition reflects index rules rather than investment merit.

items.title: 3 Stocks I'm Not Selling No Matter What the Market Does
items.summary: An investor outlines three dividend stocks he plans to hold through market downturns: Medtronic (a medical device company with 49 years of dividend increases), Realty Income (a REIT with 31 years of dividend growth and 5.2% yield), and Nucor (a steel maker with 50+ consecutive annual dividend hikes). The author argues that well-run companies remain well-run regardless of market conditions and represent solid long-term holdings.

items.title: Accenture Shares Plunged 50% This Year. Here's What Investors Need to Know.
items.summary: Accenture's stock has fallen 50% from its 2026 high due to two main factors: DOGE-driven cuts to federal contracts (8% of revenue) and investor fears that agentic AI will automate consulting work. However, the company posted record bookings of $22.1B in Q2, generated $3.6B in quarterly free cash flow, and partnered with OpenAI and Anthropic for AI deployment, suggesting the market may be pricing in excessive pessimism.

items.title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Is Sitting on Nearly $400 Billion in Cash. Is a Stock Market Crash Coming?
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway ended Q1 2026 with a record $397 billion in cash and Treasury bills, having been a net seller of stocks for over a dozen quarters. Rather than signaling an imminent market crash, the cash pile reflects CEO Greg Abel and Warren Buffett's difficulty finding attractively priced investment opportunities in an expensive market. Recent moves include an $8.5 billion acquisition of Taylor Morrison and a $10 billion investment in Alphabet, though these are modest relative to the cash position.

items.title: SpaceX's Out-of-This-World Plan Has Flaws. That Bodes Well for This Terrestial Stock.
items.summary: SpaceX plans to build orbital AI data centers to solve terrestrial energy and environmental concerns, but the concept faces significant technical and financial hurdles. Orbital data centers would cost three times more than ground-based facilities and require a 70% cost reduction to become competitive by 2040. Meanwhile, demand for terrestrial data centers will continue growing, benefiting electric utility companies like American Electric Power.

items.title: Dividend Stock Showdown: NextEra Energy vs. Dominion Energy -- Which Should You Own?
items.summary: NextEra Energy is acquiring Dominion Energy in a deal expected to take 12-18 months for regulatory approval. While the stocks are currently tied together, Dominion offers a higher dividend yield (3.9% vs 2.9%) during the waiting period, making it attractive for income-focused investors willing to accept modest downside risk if the deal fails.

items.title: Where Will Nvidia Stock Be in 2030?
items.summary: Nvidia's stock has declined 18% from its May high amid concerns about peak AI spending, despite strong Q1 FY2027 results showing 85% revenue growth. While major tech companies plan $725 billion in AI infrastructure spending for 2026, risks include slowing capital expenditure cycles, competition from in-house chips by customers, and potential margin compression. The stock trades at 30x earnings (down from 40+), and could reach $200s-$300s by 2030 if the AI buildout sustains, but faces significant downside if spending peaks.

items.title: Zoetis Inc. (NYSE: ZTS) Class Action Lawsuit Seeks Recovery for Investors; July 27, 2026, Deadline - Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP
items.summary: A securities fraud class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making materially false statements about product adoption during the period from January 14, 2025 through May 6, 2026. The complaint alleges the company misrepresented the performance of key products including Librela, Simparica Trio, Apoquel, and Cytopoint. Zoetis's stock fell 21.5% on May 7, 2026, following disappointing first quarter results showing significant decline in its Companion Animal business. Investors have until July 27, 2026, to seek lead plaintiff status.

items.title: Here's What a $50,000 Investment in SpaceX Stock Could Look Like in 10 Years, According to Billionaire Investor Ron Baron
items.summary: Billionaire investor Ron Baron, who has made $8 billion from Tesla investments, believes SpaceX could be worth 10-30 times its post-IPO value in 10-15 years. Baron is bullish on SpaceX's Starlink satellite internet service, which he expects to generate $1 trillion in annual revenue within a decade, and the company's AI and chip manufacturing initiatives. A $50,000 investment at the IPO price of $135 could potentially be worth $500,000 to $1.5 million, though several operational milestones must be achieved for these projections to materialize.

items.title: Acetic Acid Market Size to Hit $34.96 Billion by 2035, Fueled by Rising VAM Demand and Expanding Polyester Production | Report by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global acetic acid market is projected to grow from $18.57 billion in 2025 to $34.96 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 6.52%. Growth is driven by rising demand for vinyl acetate monomer (VAM) in adhesives and coatings, and expanding polyester and PET resin production. The U.S. market is expected to reach $6.73 billion by 2035, while Europe is projected to grow to $4.95 billion. Asia Pacific dominates with 54.6% market share, led by China's integrated manufacturing capabilities.

items.title: Intel vs. Navitas: Which Semiconductor Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: Intel and Navitas Semiconductor represent different semiconductor strategies: Intel is pivoting to a foundry model for chip manufacturing while Navitas specializes in next-generation power materials like gallium nitride for AI and EVs. Intel shows stronger near-term momentum with revenue growth acceleration, while Navitas faces recovery risks after exiting its Chinese consumer business to focus on AI markets.

items.title: Graphene Chip Market Size to Hit USD 19.78 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global graphene chip market, valued at USD 4.41 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to USD 19.78 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 16.20%. Growth is driven by rising demand for AI computing, advanced nanoelectronics, and high-performance semiconductors. Asia Pacific leads with 38.45% market share, while transistors and electronics applications dominate segments. Key players include Samsung, Intel, TSMC, and NVIDIA.

items.title: Solar Backsheet Market Size to Hit USD 6.91 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global Solar Backsheet Market was valued at USD 3.84 Billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 6.91 Billion by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 6.74%. Growth is driven by rising global solar installations, increased investment in renewable energy infrastructure, and development of high-efficiency photovoltaic modules. Fluoropolymer backsheets currently dominate with 51% market share, while non-fluoropolymer and recyclable alternatives are expected to grow fastest. Utility-scale solar projects and ground-mounted systems lead applications, with Asia Pacific as the dominant region.

items.title: eBay vs. Macy's: Which Consumer Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: The article compares eBay and Macy's as consumer stocks for 2026. eBay operates a capital-light global marketplace focused on niche categories with strong margins (18.3% net margin), but faces legal risks from cyberstalking lawsuits and competition from AI-powered search tools. Macy's is undergoing a turnaround strategy with lower profitability (2.8% net margin) and vulnerability to consumer spending shifts. The author concludes neither stock is an attractive investment due to their respective risk profiles.

items.title: SpaceX vs. Tesla: Here's Which Elon Musk Stock I'd Buy Right Now
items.summary: The article compares SpaceX and Tesla as investment options, both expensive stocks betting on future profitability. SpaceX, newly public, is unprofitable with $8.7 billion in losses and negative free cash flow of ~$20 billion annually, though it has $70 billion in net cash. Tesla is more stable with $98 billion in annual revenue, positive operating margins of 4.9%, and $7 billion in positive free cash flow. The author recommends Tesla as the less risky choice between two very risky stocks, despite both betting on uncertain future prospects (AI for SpaceX, robotics for Tesla).

items.title: Propionic Acid Market Projected to Reach $1.74 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global propionic acid market is projected to grow from $1.22 billion in 2025 to $1.74 billion by 2035 at a 3.63% CAGR, driven by rising demand for food preservation, animal feed additives, and pharmaceutical applications. The animal feed segment leads with 42.50% market share, while bio-based propionic acid is the fastest-growing segment at 10.81% CAGR. North America dominates with 35.80% market share, while Asia-Pacific is expected to grow at the highest rate.

items.title: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy as Demand for Custom Chips Soars
items.summary: As demand for custom AI chips rises, Broadcom and Marvell Technology are positioned to capitalize on hyperscalers' shift away from Nvidia GPUs. Broadcom reported 143% year-over-year growth in AI chip sales to $10.8 billion with over 200% growth expected next quarter, while Marvell achieved record revenue of $2.4 billion with accelerating growth expected throughout fiscal 2027. Both companies benefit from major partnerships and increasing capex spending by tech giants like Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft.

items.title: 1 Hot Industrial Stock Riding the AI Infrastructure Boom
items.summary: Comfort Systems USA (FIX) is benefiting significantly from AI data center investment demand, with a 1,160% return over three years. The company provides mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) systems for data centers. Growth drivers include surging orders, backlog expansion, margin expansion from higher-margin AI projects, and expanding modular manufacturing capacity. Trading at 45x 2026 earnings, the stock could continue rising if AI data center spending remains strong.

items.title: Brain Implants Market Size to Reach USD 18.39 Billion by 2035, Growing at 9.99% CAGR | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global brain implants market, valued at USD 7.10 billion in 2025, is projected to reach USD 18.39 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 9.99%. Growth is driven by rising prevalence of neurological disorders and increasing adoption of deep brain stimulation, neuromodulation systems, and brain-computer interfaces. North America leads the market, with the U.S. expected to grow from USD 3.16 billion to USD 8.17 billion by 2035.

items.title: The Sneaky Way SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI Can Destroy the Trump Bull Market
items.summary: Recently amended index inclusion rules allow SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI to gain fast-track entry into major stock indexes. However, historical data shows that hyped tech IPOs experience average year-one drawdowns of 55%, and game-changing technologies typically face optimization challenges that could undermine the current Trump bull market.

items.title: 3 Beaten-Down AI Chip Stocks to Consider Buying in the Sell-Off
items.summary: AI chip stocks have experienced a significant sell-off due to concerns about whether AI spending will justify industry investments. The article examines three beaten-down chip stocks—Nvidia, ON Semiconductor, and Intel—as potential buying opportunities. Nvidia trades at 29x earnings with strong 85% revenue growth; ON Semiconductor fell 23% after announcing a $7 billion acquisition but shows signs of cyclical recovery; Intel has surged on turnaround efforts but remains unprofitable on a GAAP basis. The author favors Nvidia as the least risky of the three.

items.title: From $135 to $154: Tracking SpaceX's Stock Volatility in Its First Weeks as a Public Company
items.summary: SpaceX experienced extreme volatility in its first weeks as a public company, surging 50% to $226 before plummeting 32% to $155. The wild swings are driven by its tiny 4.2% public float, with 95.8% of shares locked up by insiders. A $25 billion debt offering announcement on June 22 triggered the recent decline. The stock is expected to remain volatile until the lockup period ends in December.

items.title: Qualcomm Just Nearly Doubled Its Most Important Growth Target, Confirming Its Place as a Key AI Stock
items.summary: Qualcomm nearly doubled its non-handset revenue target to $40 billion by fiscal 2029 and set a $15 billion data center revenue goal, marking its boldest diversification push yet. The company secured a multi-year deal with Meta for its new Dragonfly C1000 processor, though it faces significant competition from Nvidia in the data center market. Trading at a modest 17x earnings, the stock offers potential upside if Qualcomm can execute on its ambitious growth plans.

items.title: What Investors Should Know About Wendy's Sudden Surge
items.summary: Wendy's stock surged 42% this week due to a viral Reddit WallStreetBets rally, similar to GameStop and AMC meme-stock phenomena. While the spike is driven by social media momentum, the company has legitimate turnaround potential with new CFO Steve Cirulis (who previously helped turn around Potbelly) and expansion plans in China. However, the company faces challenges including declining foot traffic and prolonged same-store sales slumps, making long-term success dependent on execution rather than viral trends.

items.title: After Issuing Its First Stock Split in 2020, Tesla Took Just 2 Years to Issue Its Second Split. Could a Third Stock Split Come in 2026?
items.summary: Tesla is unlikely to conduct a third stock split in 2026 despite splitting its stock in August 2020 and August 2022. With shares trading below $400, the stock price would need to nearly triple to meet historical conditions that preceded previous splits. Additionally, fractional share investing availability has reduced the necessity for splits.

items.title: Best Buy(BBY.US) Insider Sells US$32.7 Million in Common Stock - Moomoo
items.summary: Best Buy(BBY.US) Insider Sells US$32.7 Million in Common Stock    Moomoo

items.title: The Lemon Reps Recover Over $3.2 Million for Lemon Law Victims in Their First 12 Months
items.summary: The Lemon Reps, a Beverly Hills-based lemon law firm, announced it recovered over $3.2 million for clients and resolved more than 1,500 cases in the past year with an average 90-day settlement timeline. General Motors and Stellantis vehicles accounted for the largest share of claims, with common issues including transmission failures, electrical defects, powertrain problems, and brake/drivetrain complaints.

items.title: AI ROI Debate Misses the Value of Owning Critical Infrastructure
items.summary: The article argues that the AI infrastructure buildout debate should focus less on immediate ROI and more on the strategic value of owning critical infrastructure. Global AI capex is projected at $5.5 trillion through 2030, with hyperscaler spending reaching $650-697 billion in 2026. Hyperscalers are generating returns today while funding future layers, and access to capital is becoming a competitive moat. The real ROI question belongs to model-layer companies like Anthropic and OpenAI, which mirror early-stage cloud players. The winners will be companies with the cheapest path to funding the next wave, not necessarily those with the best technology.

items.title: UnitedHealth is Near its 52-Week High. Wall Street Is Starting to Notice This Blue Chip Stock -- and So Should You.
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) has surged 25% this year and is trading near its 52-week high. The healthcare giant offers revenue growth, a solid 2.3% dividend yield with 17 consecutive years of increases, and resilience against economic downturns. The company is deploying a $1.5 billion AI initiative to reduce administrative costs and improve margins, while managing rising medical costs through responsive pricing strategies. Despite valuation concerns and industry-wide medical cost pressures, UnitedHealth's diversified business model and strong cash flow position it as an attractive investment for income and total-return focused investors.

items.title: GitLab vs. Snowflake: Which Technology Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: The article compares GitLab and Snowflake as investment options for 2026. GitLab, a DevSecOps platform, reported $955.2M revenue (25.8% growth) with improving profitability and zero debt. Snowflake, an AI Data Cloud platform, generated $4.7B revenue (29.2% growth) with $1.1B free cash flow but higher losses and debt. The author recommends Snowflake due to its critical role in providing data for AI systems, despite its higher valuation premium.

items.title: Nasdaq Selloff Shows AI Leadership Is Turning Into Market Drag
items.summary: The Nasdaq is experiencing its fourth consecutive losing session as AI-related stocks face pressure from multiple headwinds: OpenAI's delayed IPO to 2027, surging memory chip costs forcing price increases across the hardware industry, and concerns about the sustainability of AI infrastructure spending. While memory chip makers benefit from tight supply, device manufacturers like Apple are forced to raise prices, signaling margin compression across the tech ecosystem. The Dow, meanwhile, reaches record highs on strength in non-AI sectors like industrials and healthcare, highlighting a significant rotation away from crowded growth stocks.

items.title: Is the Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) a Buy Before July 10?
items.summary: SK Hynix plans to list American depositary receipts on Nasdaq on July 10, targeting $29.4 billion in proceeds for chip fabrication expansion. The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) provides indirect exposure to SK Hynix and other AI memory leaders. While the listing is a meaningful catalyst, the article advises against timing purchases around this event, recommending dollar-cost averaging instead as the market has already priced in the expected development.

items.title: MDA Space Targets US Defense Market With $620M Acquisition
items.summary: MDA Space is positioning itself as a cross-border defense champion through a $620 million acquisition of Blue Canyon Technologies from RTX Corp and a C$688 million satellite contract with the Canadian Space Agency. Despite strong Q1 2026 earnings (32.2% YoY revenue growth), the stock trades at a significant valuation discount compared to peers. The Blue Canyon acquisition provides access to US defense contracts through localized assets and security clearances, unlocking a $3.5 billion addressable pipeline. Recent price weakness is attributed to derivative-driven hedging rather than fundamental concerns.

items.title: Chasing Alphabet's 6.25% Dividend Yield? Read the Fine Print First.
items.summary: Alphabet issued $16.75 billion in convertible preferred stock with a 6.25% dividend yield as part of an $85 billion capital raise to fund AI infrastructure expansion. However, the preferred shares convert to common stock in May 2029, after which investors would receive only the 0.25% common dividend yield. The article advises long-term investors to own common shares instead, as the preferred stock's attractiveness depends on bearish views of Alphabet's stock performance over the next five years.

items.title: The Memory Shortage Is Minting Winners. 3 Stocks Not Named Micron That Could Cash In.
items.summary: A severe memory chip shortage driven by AI buildout is creating windfall profits for storage companies. SanDisk, Western Digital, and Seagate are all cashing in through soaring prices and locked-in multiyear contracts. SanDisk offers the most direct exposure to the shortage with 97% sequential revenue growth, while Western Digital and Seagate benefit indirectly as customers turn to hard drives when SSDs become prohibitively expensive.

items.title: Cathie Wood Goes Bargain Hunting: 3 Stocks She Just Bought
items.summary: Ark Invest CEO Cathie Wood added to positions in Palantir, Cerebras Systems, and Coinbase Global on Thursday after all three stocks declined at least 5%. Palantir has fallen 48% from its November peak despite accelerating revenue growth and strong earnings. Cerebras, a recent AI chip IPO, has surrendered over half its value but shows impressive technology potential. Coinbase faces headwinds from declining cryptocurrency interest, with revenue down significantly in recent quarters.

items.title: Eaton vs. Vertiv: Which Industrials Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: Eaton and Vertiv are both benefiting from AI data center demand and electrification trends, but offer different value propositions. Eaton provides diversified power management across industrial, aerospace, and electrical sectors with more conservative valuation (P/E 30.4x), while Vertiv specializes in high-growth digital infrastructure with stronger recent growth (27.7% revenue growth) but higher valuation (P/E 49.1x). The article recommends Eaton for its breadth, stability, and dividend history, though Vertiv presents a compelling growth opportunity.

items.title: Beyond Rockets: SpaceX's Real Growth Story Is Its AI Infrastructure Business
items.summary: SpaceX's primary growth opportunity lies in AI infrastructure rather than rockets, with a $26.5 trillion addressable market in AI. The company has secured major compute capacity rental deals with Anthropic ($1.2B/month), Alphabet ($920M/month), and Reflection AI ($150M/month). SpaceX plans to launch up to 1 million solar-powered orbital data centers starting in 2028, with AI revenue projected to grow from $3.2 billion in 2025 to $322 billion by 2030, though significant capital expenditures ($12.7B in 2025) are required.

items.title: NICE vs. Workiva: Which Technology Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: NICE and Workiva are compared as enterprise software investments. NICE offers profitable AI-powered customer service automation with strong cash flow and zero debt, but faces slowing growth and competitive pressures. Workiva shows accelerating revenue growth (19.7%) and crossed $1B in revenue, but remains unprofitable with concentration risk from SEC filing dependency. The author recommends Workiva for 2026 due to its momentum and attractive valuation after a pullback, despite NICE's financial stability.

items.summary: Merck to Acquire Bio-Techne in Cash Deal    The Globe and Mail

items.title: Abacus Global Management’s Defamation Suit Against Coventry and its Chairman Alan Buerger Advances in U.S. District Court
items.summary: A U.S. District Court in Florida denied Coventry First LLC's motion to dismiss in a defamation lawsuit filed by Abacus Global Management. The court found that Abacus plausibly alleged claims of defamation and anticompetitive conduct, allowing the case to proceed with depositions of Coventry executives. Abacus alleges Coventry engaged in a years-long campaign to disseminate false information about the company to manipulate market sentiment and is seeking hundreds of millions in damages.

items.title: Here's Why Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) May Be the Smartest AI Infrastructure Buy Right Now
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) is positioned as the premier AI infrastructure investment, controlling over 70% of the global foundry market. The company benefits from strong AI chip demand, high margins (47% net profit margin), and significant switching costs for customers. With stock price up 110% over the past year and Q1 revenue surging 40% year-over-year, TSMC is raising prices amid strong demand, reinforcing its competitive moat despite growing competition from Intel and Samsung.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Faces an AI Reality Check as Valuation Concerns Mount
items.summary: Technology stocks face a significant pullback as investors reassess stretched valuations and rising AI infrastructure costs. The Nasdaq 100 has fallen over 4% this week, with Apple leading declines after announcing price increases due to higher semiconductor costs. Concerns mount over whether massive AI investments can generate sufficient near-term profits, compounded by a more hawkish Federal Reserve outlook that could increase borrowing costs.

items.title: J&J CEO Now Sees 'Line of Sight' to Double-Digit Growth for His $100 Billion Company. Here's Why Investors Should Pay Attention.
items.summary: Johnson & Johnson's CEO Joaquin Duato projects double-digit growth for the $100 billion healthcare company, supported by 28 platforms generating $1 billion each in revenue. The company plans to invest $55 billion in the U.S. over four years, including a new biologics manufacturing facility in North Carolina, leveraging favorable tax policies to drive growth in cancer, immune-mediated, and neurological disease treatments.

items.title: Merck KGaA (MKGAF) Acquires Bio-Techne (TECH) for $73 per Share - GuruFocus
items.summary: Merck KGaA (MKGAF) Acquires Bio-Techne (TECH) for $73 per Share    GuruFocus

items.title: Wall Street Scrapped a 25-Year-Old Rule. Does It Make Robinhood Stock a Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Regulators have eliminated the pattern day trader (PDT) rule that previously restricted retail accounts with less than $25,000 from making frequent day trades. The change, effective June 4, 2026, replaces the $25,000 equity requirement with a $2,000 minimum and risk-based margin system. This development particularly benefits Robinhood Markets, whose user base (average account balance ~$4,000) was heavily constrained by the old rule. The removal could unlock increased trading volumes and boost Robinhood's transaction-based revenues through payment for order flow and margin interest, though the stock is already priced at 46x forward earnings.

items.title: Coaxial Cable Market Size to Hit USD 46.24 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global coaxial cable market, valued at $21.58 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to $46.24 billion by 2035 at a 7.94% CAGR. Growth is driven by rising broadband demand, high-speed internet expansion, and 5G deployment. North America leads with 34.8% market share, while Asia Pacific is expected to grow fastest at 10.12% CAGR. Key segments include video distribution (48.5% share) and internet data transfer (fastest growing at 10.89% CAGR).

items.title: Carnival Stock Posts Record Quarter, But Guidance Spooks Investors
items.summary: Carnival reported record second-quarter results with $6.66 billion in revenue and adjusted net income up 21% year-over-year, despite higher fuel costs. However, weak forward guidance citing Middle East tensions and demand concerns spooked investors, causing the stock to drop 5%. The company maintains strong fundamentals with record customer deposits of $9 billion and continued debt reduction, but faces headwinds from geopolitical risks and energy costs.

items.title: Data Center Infrastructure Management Market Size to Hit $12.82 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) market is valued at $3.61 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $12.82 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 13.51%. Growth is driven by AI infrastructure investments, sustainability regulations, multi-cloud adoption, and demand for real-time monitoring solutions. The U.S. market is expected to grow from $0.95 billion to $3.38 billion, while Europe is projected to reach $4.16 billion by 2035.

items.title: Cable Assembly Market Size to Hit $348.66 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global cable assembly market is projected to grow from USD 194.54 billion in 2025 to USD 348.66 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 6.03%. Growth is driven by rising demand for high-speed connectivity, 5G networks, cloud computing, and digital infrastructure expansion. Custom cable assemblies and fiber-optic assemblies lead in market segments, while Asia Pacific dominates regionally with 38.46% market share.

items.title: Bloodstream Infection Testing Market Size to Reach USD 1,404.68 Million by 2035 | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global bloodstream infection testing market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 4.80% through 2035, driven by rising sepsis cases, antimicrobial stewardship mandates, and adoption of rapid molecular diagnostics. North America leads the market, with the U.S. contributing 87.4% of regional revenues, while Asia Pacific shows the fastest growth. Key technologies include molecular diagnostics and whole blood testing, which are rapidly replacing traditional blood culture methods.

items.title: S&P 500: Is The Market Finally Questioning the AI Spending Trade?
items.summary: The S&P 500 finished flat as the market rotates between mega-cap tech and semiconductor stocks. Apple's announcement of price hikes signals that AI hardware costs are squeezing margins, raising questions about whether major AI capex spenders will scale back spending. The software-to-hardware ratio (IGV/SMH) shows hardware currently winning, while the financial sector shows potential bear pennant formation.

items.title: Observability Tools and Platforms Market Size to Surpass $9.07 Billion by 2035 as Cloud-Native Infrastructure and AI-Powered Monitoring Drive Adoption | SNS Insider
items.summary: The global observability tools and platforms market is projected to grow from $3.14 billion in 2025 to $9.07 billion by 2035, driven by cloud-native infrastructure adoption, AI-powered monitoring, and regulatory compliance requirements. The U.S. market is expected to reach $2.37 billion while Europe is projected to hit $4.21 billion by 2035. Solutions dominate with 69% market share, while cloud deployment accounts for 65% of the market.

items.title: 2 Excellent Stocks to Buy on the Dip
items.summary: Netflix and Shopify have experienced significant stock declines due to company-specific concerns and broader industry headwinds, but both companies maintain strong financial performance and competitive advantages. Netflix benefits from its dominant streaming position, growing user base of 325+ million subscribers, and AI-powered innovations, while Shopify shows accelerating revenue growth of 34% year-over-year and improving profitability metrics despite a steep valuation. Both stocks are positioned as attractive buying opportunities for long-term investors.

items.title: Could This Be Amazon's Next Big Opportunity?
items.summary: Amazon is exploring selling its Trainium custom AI chips to external customers, potentially creating a major new revenue stream. CEO Andy Jassy estimates the AI chips business could reach $50 billion in annual run rate currently and exceed $100 billion within years. This initiative, combined with Amazon's strong performance in AWS, advertising, and e-commerce, positions the company for continued growth as it leverages AI across its diversified business segments.

items.title: 2 Beaten-Down Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks for Contrarian Investors to Consider Buying
items.summary: The article examines two AI-related stocks that have experienced significant declines: Recursion Pharmaceuticals, an AI-focused biotech company using algorithms for drug discovery with promising candidates but no approved products yet, and Fiverr, a freelance platform shifting strategy toward complex, higher-value projects to counter revenue and user declines. Both present contrarian investment opportunities with substantial upside potential but carry considerable risks.

items.title: FDA Panel Backs Moderna’s mRNA-Based Influenza Vaccine for Adults 50+ - Infectious Disease Advisor
items.summary: FDA Panel Backs Moderna’s mRNA-Based Influenza Vaccine for Adults 50+    Infectious Disease Advisor

items.title: Why Apple Stock Fell Today
items.summary: Apple stock dropped 6.41% after the company raised prices on Macs and iPads to offset surging memory and storage chip costs driven by AI demand. CEO Tim Cook stated the cost increases have become unsustainable. Meanwhile, memory chip maker Micron reported strong earnings and expects supply constraints to persist beyond 2027.

items.title: Why Qualcomm Stock Popped Today
items.summary: Qualcomm shares rose 3.65% after the chipmaker unveiled new AI-focused growth targets at its 2026 Investor Day. The company introduced its Dragonfly C1000 CPU designed for agentic AI workloads, with Meta Platforms already signed on as a customer. Qualcomm expects non-handset revenue to reach $40 billion in fiscal 2029 (double prior forecast) and targets over $18 in adjusted earnings per share.

items.title: Stock Market Today, June 25: Bio-Techne Surges After Merck KGaA Announces $73-Per-Share Cash Acquisition Offer
items.summary: Bio-Techne shares surged 20% after Merck KGaA announced a $73-per-share cash acquisition offer. The deal comes one week after activist investor Ananym Capital Management built a stake and pushed for a sale. Trading volume reached 51.3M shares, significantly above average. Merck KGaA expects to achieve over $150 million in annual cost synergies within three years.

items.title: Why Delek Holdings Rallied Today
items.summary: Delek U.S. stock rallied 6% on Thursday following a fire at the Trainer refinery in Pennsylvania, owned by Delta Air Lines' subsidiary Monroe Energy. The incident is expected to tighten jet fuel supply and boost margins for competing refiners like Delek, which has a high percentage of revenue from jet fuel. The rally comes as investors had anticipated margin compression following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire, but the refinery outage could offset that decline.

items.title: Nvidia’s Micron-Led Bounce Tests Confidence in the AI Capex Cycle
items.summary: Nvidia rebounded near $200 after Micron's record earnings reignited confidence in AI semiconductor demand. CEO Jensen Huang declared AI has entered a profitability era and confirmed the next-generation Vera Rubin architecture is in full production. However, the stock faces headwinds from rising competition, margin pressure from memory suppliers, geopolitical risks from China export controls, and uncertainty about whether AI capital spending can sustain current growth rates.

items.title: ZOETIS DEADLINE: ROSEN, GLOBAL INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Zoetis Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action – ZTS
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements about its Companion Animal products during January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026. The lawsuit claims the company failed to disclose weakening veterinarian adoption of its Librela pain treatment following FDA safety warnings, significant market share losses in Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining market share in dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. The lead plaintiff deadline is July 27, 2026.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy With $3,000 Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks as buying opportunities amid recent market weakness: S&P Global, which is undergoing strategic divestitures to focus on core high-demand businesses; Qualcomm, positioned to benefit from the growing edge AI computing market; and Chewy, an online pet supplies retailer with strong recurring revenue despite near-term consumer headwinds.

items.title: Darden Restaurants (DRI) Reports Strong Q4 Earnings and Positive Outlook for 2027 - GuruFocus
items.summary: Darden Restaurants (DRI) Reports Strong Q4 Earnings and Positive Outlook for 2027    GuruFocus

items.title: Lululemon Athletica vs. Nike: What Revenue Trends Reveal for These Sportswear Stocks
items.summary: Nike maintains significantly higher revenue than Lululemon Athletica, with both companies showing consistent seasonal patterns. However, both face challenges with flat or reduced growth outlooks. Lululemon reduced its 2026 sales guidance to flat, while Nike's Q3 revenue was flat year-over-year. Both stocks have dropped near 52-week lows, though Lululemon shows more quarters with year-over-year growth. Nike offers a dividend yield of 3.9% with 24 consecutive years of increases.

items.title: 7 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Trading at Attractive Valuations
items.summary: The article identifies seven U.S. dividend stocks offering yields between 4.1% and 5.9% with 19-56 years of continuous dividend payments and valuations undervalued by 21.9% to 70.7%. It emphasizes that successful dividend investing requires evaluating payout ratios, dividend consistency, growth history, financial fundamentals, and valuation metrics rather than relying solely on yield. Two highlighted examples are Sonoco Products (50 consecutive years of dividend increases, 4.3% yield, 10x forward P/E) and McCormick & Company (102 consecutive years of dividend payments, 9.1% average annual payout growth).

items.title: Prediction: You Won't Recognize ExxonMobil in 2040
items.summary: ExxonMobil is positioning itself for significant transformation by 2040 through technology-driven efficiency improvements. The company forecasts $25 billion in earnings growth and $35 billion in cash flow increases through 2030 without major spending increases, targeting a 17% return on deployed capital. With a 43-year dividend increase streak and strong operational performance in key assets like Guyana and the Permian Basin, ExxonMobil aims to become a leaner, more efficient energy company while maintaining its dividend commitment.

items.title: How Intel Is Packaging the Future of American Chips
items.summary: Intel has surged 281.8% year-to-date by pivoting to become a sovereign foundry powerhouse, with a new advanced packaging division led by SK hynix veteran Seok-Hee Lee. The company secured preliminary agreements with Apple for M-series chip production and a $5 billion strategic investment from NVIDIA. However, Intel trades at a stretched 223x forward P/E ratio with massive operating deficits, requiring flawless execution and near-term margin compression to justify current valuations.

items.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges First Solar, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against First Solar, Inc. alleging that company executives made materially false statements and failed to disclose material facts regarding the company's ability to manage U.S. tariff policy impacts. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased First Solar securities between February 26, 2025 and February 24, 2026. Investors have until August 24, 2026 to request lead plaintiff status.

items.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Zoetis Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Zoetis Inc. alleging securities fraud for making false statements about product growth and market share in its Companion Animal segment. The complaint claims the company failed to disclose weakening adoption of Librela due to FDA safety warnings, market share losses for Simparica Trio to competitors, and declining sales of dermatology products Apoquel and Cytopoint. The class period covers January 14, 2025 to May 6, 2026, with a lead plaintiff deadline of July 27, 2026.

items.title: This Might Be the Cheapest Meta Platforms Trades in Years. Here's Why.
items.summary: Meta Platforms trades at the lowest P/E ratio among Magnificent Seven stocks while growing faster than most peers, with revenue up 33% YoY and operating income up 30% YoY. Despite a 10% YTD decline, the company's 20.5 P/E ratio appears undervalued compared to the S&P 500's 32.2. Beyond its core advertising business, Meta is diversifying into AI through Superintelligence Labs, which could unlock new revenue streams and potentially drive significant stock appreciation.

items.title: Nano Nuclear Energy vs. NuScale Power: Which Nuclear Energy Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: The article compares two nuclear energy companies: Nano Nuclear Energy, which develops portable microreactors for military and industrial use, and NuScale Power, which focuses on larger small modular reactors for utilities. Both are unprofitable pre-commercial ventures facing significant risks. NuScale is recommended as the better 2026 buy due to its regulatory approval and faster path to commercialization, though Nano's HALEU fuel technology may offer long-term advantages.

items.title: Forget SpaceX. I'm Loading Up on This Genius Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Instead.
items.summary: The article argues that Nvidia is a superior investment compared to SpaceX, citing the company's dominance in AI computing and expected growth in 2027. Nvidia's new Rubin chip architecture promises significant cost reductions for AI inference and training, while hyperscalers are expected to increase data center spending to $1 trillion in 2027. The stock trades at a reasonable valuation of 23x forward earnings, with potential upside as the market reprices the growth opportunity.

items.title: Micron Prints Perfect, and Now the Chart Has to Answer
items.summary: Micron delivered exceptional Q3 results with $41.5B revenue (up 346% YoY), 84.9% gross margin, and EPS of $25.11 beating expectations. The company guided Q4 to $50B revenue and secured $100B in long-term take-or-pay contracts through 2030, providing structural support. However, the stock's reaction remains uncertain as it trades within a rising channel, with the key question being whether the market views this as peak growth or a sign of continued acceleration.

items.title: Micron and Qualcomm Show AI Demand Is Broadening Beyond GPUs
items.summary: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday following strong earnings and guidance from Micron Technology and Qualcomm, which revived confidence in the AI trade despite sticky inflation. Micron's fiscal Q3 results significantly beat expectations with EPS of $25.11 versus forecasts of $20.78 and revenue of $41.46 billion. Qualcomm doubled its 2029 non-handset revenue forecast to $40 billion, signaling that AI demand is expanding beyond GPUs into data centers and other sectors.

items.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
items.summary: SpaceX shares have fallen nearly 20% from their June 16 peak after surging 67% at IPO. Despite the dip, the author recommends against buying, citing an extremely high price-to-sales ratio of 77 (compared to Nvidia's 20 and Micron's 24), lack of profitability, and concerns about planned debt and equity dilution. The stock is priced for perfection with no margin of safety.

items.title: Winning AI Search: A Marketer’s Guide to Press Releases and Earned Media
items.summary: The American Marketing Association and Notified are hosting a webinar on June 30, 2026, to discuss how press releases and earned media help brands achieve visibility in AI-powered search results. The session will cover how to optimize content for AI discoverability and cite a case study of revenue growth through AI-optimized press releases.

items.title: Salesforce vs. ServiceNow: What Do Their Revenue Trends Tell Investors?
items.summary: Salesforce and ServiceNow demonstrate strong revenue growth despite 2026 SaaS sector concerns about AI disruption. Salesforce maintains a larger revenue base with 13% year-over-year growth in Q1 2026, while ServiceNow shows faster growth at 22% year-over-year. Both companies' expanding revenues suggest AI is acting as a growth catalyst rather than a threat to their business models.

items.title: 1 Metric That Highlights Apple's Focus on AI
items.summary: Apple is accelerating its R&D spending at 33.6% year-over-year, outpacing its 16.6% revenue growth, signaling serious commitment to AI development. While the market views Apple as lagging peers in AI infrastructure spending, the company's strategy leverages its 2.5 billion active devices to drive product and service revenue growth rather than competing on raw infrastructure investment.

items.title: Lycia Therapeutics Raises $75 Million in Oversubscribed Financing and Strengthens Leadership Team with Appointment of Chief Financial Officer and General Counsel
items.summary: Lycia Therapeutics completed an oversubscribed $75 million Series D financing round co-led by Janus Henderson Investors and Balyasny Asset Management. The company appointed Amy Bachrodt as CFO and promoted Karen Flick to General Counsel. Proceeds will support clinical development of LCA-0061 for food allergy and LCA-0321 for Graves' disease.

items.title: Explosive Detectors Market Size to Worth USD 37.05 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global explosive detectors market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 12.60% from 2026 to 2035, driven by rising security threats, airport infrastructure expansion, and geopolitical tensions. Handheld detectors and Ion Mobility Spectrometry technology dominate the market, while airports account for 46.25% of end-user demand. North America leads globally, with Asia-Pacific emerging as the fastest-growing region.

items.title: AMD vs. Intel: Which Stock Is the Better Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Both AMD and Intel have surged amid AI chip demand, with Intel up 258% in 2026 backed by $9B in federal government support and a new Apple partnership. However, Intel's valuation is extremely expensive with P/E ratios of 161 and 904, while AMD offers stronger fundamentals with 38% revenue growth and a more reasonable valuation. AMD appears the safer choice, though Intel could outperform if government support continues.

items.title: Nvidia Should Be on Your Buy List if You Are This Type of Investor
items.summary: Nvidia, with its $4.9 trillion market cap, is now positioned to attract younger, risk-averse investors seeking growth without excessive risk. The company demonstrates strong fundamentals including 85% revenue growth, a modest P/E ratio of 32, and $80 billion in liquidity. While its low dividend yield (0.14%) makes it less attractive to income investors, Nvidia's recent 2,400% dividend increase signals growing commitment to shareholders, making it suitable for conservative growth investors.

items.title: All Banks Crushed the Fed's Stress Tests This Year, and Some Truly Excelled
items.summary: All 32 banks tested in the Federal Reserve's annual stress tests passed with flying colors, demonstrating resilience against severe economic scenarios including 39% commercial property collapse, 30% home price decline, and 10% unemployment. Charles Schwab led with a 32% stressed capital ratio, while JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo all maintained healthy capital levels. Banks are expected to announce dividend increases and share buyback programs following the results.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway's Greg Abel Just Bought 3 Million Shares of Macy's Stock. It Sure Looks Cheap, but Is It a Great Value?
items.summary: Greg Abel, Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO, purchased 3 million shares of Macy's in his first quarter, marking the company's first investment in the department store retailer. Macy's trades at a P/E ratio of 10 and offers a 3% dividend yield. The company showed strong Q1 performance with 3% comparable sales growth, particularly driven by its luxury brands Bloomingdale's and Bluemercury. While the stock appears cheap, the article questions whether it represents a true value investment aligned with Berkshire's long-term philosophy.

items.title: Cloud Discovery Market Size to Surpass $9.77 Billion by 2035 as Multi-Cloud Adoption and Shadow IT Challenges Drive Enterprise Demand | Report by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global cloud discovery market is projected to grow from $1.92 billion in 2025 to $9.77 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 17.24%. Growth is driven by multi-cloud complexity, shadow IT risks, and regulatory requirements. North America leads with 43% market share, while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Public cloud and security/compliance applications dominate current segments.

items.title: Meet the Growth Stock That's Cheaper Than 6 "Magnificent Seven" Stocks. Here's Why It's a No-Brainer Buy Before the End of June.
items.summary: Netflix stock has fallen 22.3% year-to-date and now trades at a cheaper valuation than six of the Magnificent Seven stocks. Despite concerns about acquisition attempts and price hikes, the article argues Netflix remains an excellent buy due to its strong content pipeline, international revenue diversification, and low forward P/E ratio of 20.2x compared to the S&P 500's 22.4x. The company is positioned as a non-AI growth stock offering portfolio diversification.

items.title: System on Module Market Size to Hit USD 6.81 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global System on Module (SOM) market, valued at USD 3.00 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to USD 6.81 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 8.57%. Growth is driven by expanding IoT adoption, edge AI technologies, and industrial automation. ARM Architecture dominates with 72.35% market share, while COM-HPC is the fastest-growing standard. North America leads regionally, with Asia-Pacific showing the fastest growth.

items.title: Can Oracle Become a $1 Trillion Company?
items.summary: Oracle's path to a $1 trillion valuation hinges on its booming cloud infrastructure segment, which grew 93% year-over-year and now represents 30% of revenue. The company is aggressively investing $70 billion in AI infrastructure and laid off 13% of its workforce to fund this strategy. However, Oracle's $638 billion in remaining performance obligations—including a $300 billion OpenAI contract—faces significant risk, as OpenAI is currently unprofitable and burning cash at unsustainable rates, raising questions about its ability to fulfill the agreement.
